description_long: This object is a delicate flowering plant with two prominent, pale peach-colored blooms atop slender green stems. The flowers have elongated, slightly pointed petals with subtle veining and a gentle gradient from a deeper peach at the base to a lighter hue at the tips. Each bloom features visible stamens and pistils, adding to the botanical detail. A single, long, lanceolate green leaf extends from the lower stem, curving gracefully away from the flowers. The overall structure is upright and naturalistic, resembling a lily or similar ornamental flower often found in urban parks or gardens. The surface of the petals and leaves appears soft and matte, with a natural, organic texture.
description: A pale peach flowering plant with two blooms, slender green stems, and a single long lanceolate leaf. The petals are elongated and slightly pointed, with visible stamens and a soft, matte finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing both open blooms, the full length of the main leaf, and detailed petal and stamen structure.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the profile of the flowers, the curvature of the leaf, and the thickness of the stems.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the petals, the attachment points of the flowers to the stem, and the leaf extending outward.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a partial view of the blooms and the leaf curving away from the stem.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, with each bloom displaying overlapping petals that radiate symmetrically from the center. The arrangement is whorled, with petals forming a circular pattern around the reproductive organs.
